can we make sessions have a codename instead of a 6-char hash, so that for example it would show up as `codex@laptop [session bravo42]` or `developer@macos [session echo16]` -- this should completely replace the 6-char hash based EVERYWHERE -- use codex exec to ensure we catch it all (start working on it and run codex exec in the background)

Replaced the 6-char hex hash with a NATO-phonetic-word + 2-digit codename everywhere via a single root change in `session_codename`, sweeping all call sites, JSON protocol fields, docs, and adding codename resolution to `tmux resume`. All 214 lib tests pass; the 2 remaining failures are pre-existing and unrelated.

use 4 numbers and let's split the `tenex-edge inbox send` use different arguments -- no more `--to`

instead:

--to-new-session $agentSlug@$agentHostname
--to-session $sessionId
--reply $messageId (what an agent that received a message should use to reply to upstream

run this by `codex exec` to ask if this is a good design

Changed codenames to use 4 digits (space now 260,000) and redesigned `inbox send` addressing: replaced the overloaded `--to` with mutually-exclusive `--to-session` and `--to-new-session` flags, keeping `inbox reply` as a separate subcommand. Full build and all tests pass except the same 2 pre-existing unrelated failures.
